log_entries.thought_preview: The sample is a PE32 executable, likely a spreader, with an overlay. It's available in three sandboxes. I should get more information about the network activity to see if it communicates with any C2 s

There's no network activity, which is unusual for a spreader. This might indicate that it's a local-only threat or uses a different communication method. I should investigate the registry changes to s

The sample doesn't seem to attempt to persist on the system through the registry. This is another unusual behavior for a spreader. I should investigate the dropped files to see if it tries to write an

The sample drops two files, which is consistent with the process tree showing two instances of the executable. I should investigate the process activity to see if it tries to load any suspicious modul

The processes created are the two instances of the executable. The notable modules loaded are all legitimate Windows modules, which doesn't indicate any suspicious behavior. I should investigate the C
The CAPA behaviors indicate that the sample accesses the PEB, encodes data using XOR, and contains a loop. These behaviors are consistent with the initial observations. I should now form a hypothesis
